sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image:
- Green at (19, 36) is selected (dark border)
- Cross appears at (19, 24) - to the left
- Other greens: (25, 42), (37, 42)
So now I have 3 greens: (19, 36), (25, 42), (37, 42). The green at (19, 30) was consumed when I moved it to (19, 36).
Wait, but earlier I had 4 greens at (19, 30), (19, 36), (25, 42), (37, 42). I selected (19, 30) and clicked the cross at (19, 36). The green at (19, 30) moved to (19, 36), consuming the green there. So now there's 1 green at (19, 36) plus 2 bottom ones = 3 total. That matches.
Now the cross is at (19, 24). If I click it, the green moves from (19, 36) to (19, 24) without consuming anything (no green there). This would leave 3 greens at (19, 24), (25, 42), (37, 42).
But this doesn't seem to be making progress. I need to figure out the goal.
Let me think about what patterns might be the goal:
1. All greens in one panel: Currently 1 in top, 2 in bottom.
2. All greens in one column: Currently 2 in column 42, 1 in column 36.
3. All greens in one row: Currently 1 in row 19, 1 in row 25, 1 in row 37.
4. Exactly N greens remaining: Currently 3.
What if the goal is to have all greens in column 42? I need to move the top green to column 42. But the cross appears at (19, 24), not at a position in column 42.
Wait, maybe I need to move the green to a position where it can then move to column 42. Or maybe I need to select a different green.
Actually, let me reconsider. The cross appears at (19, 24) when I select (19, 36). This is to the left. The distance from (19, 36) to (19, 24) is 12 columns. Previously, when I selected (19, 30), the cross appeared at (19, 36), which is 6 columns to the right.
Hmm, maybe the cross always appears at a position that's symmetric to another green's position relative to the selected green? Or maybe it appears at a position that's a fixed distance away?
Let me think about this more carefully. When I selected (19, 30), the cross appeared at (19, 36). The other top green was at (19, 36). So the cross appeared at the position of the other green.
Now I selected (19, 36), and the cross appeared at (19, 24). There's no green at (19, 24). So the cross doesn't always appear at another green's position.
Wait, but earlier when I selected (19, 18), the cross appeared at (19, 30). There was no green at (19, 30) at that time (the green was at (19, 24)). So the cross appeared at a position between the selected green and the next green.
Actually, let me reconsider. When I selected (19, 18), the other top greens were at (19, 24) and (19, 36). The cross appeared at (19, 30), which is between (19, 24) and (19, 36). When I clicked it, the green moved to (19, 30) and consumed the green at (19, 24).
So the cross appears at a position that's between the selected green and the nearest other green in that direction. When clicked, the green moves there and consumes any greens in between.
Now, with the green at (19, 36), the cross appears at (19, 24). There are no other greens in the top panel, so the cross appears at a position to the left. If I click it, the green moves to (19, 24) without consuming anything.
This doesn't seem helpful for the goal. Let me try a different approach.
What if I select one of the bottom greens and see if a cross appears? Earlier when I selected (25, 42), no cross appeared. But maybe I need to select the other bottom green, (37, 42).
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the goal. What if the goal is to have all greens in the bottom panel? Currently there's 1 in the top and 2 in the bottom. If I could move the top green to the bottom panel...
But the cross only appears in the same row (horizontal movement) for the top panel. How do I move a green from the top panel to the bottom panel?
Maybe I need to use the cross to move the green to a position where it can then move vertically? Or maybe there's a different mechanic I'm missing.
Let me try selecting (37, 42) and see if a cross appears.
